right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 541 KDR-melanoma evidence rows (MONDO_0005105); top rows are europepmc literature (resourceScore 133 and 175) plus Cancer Gene Census somatic-mutation evidence linking KDR to desmoplastic, nodular and superficial-spreading melanoma subtypes, all at association score 1. GWAS mapped 50 SNPs to the KDR locus at 4q12 (e.g. rs113828443, intron_variant), but the records carry no trait/phenotype fields, so no direct melanoma germline association is demonstrated. Strong somatic/literature link, no causal germline signal.
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource (541 evidences, datasources europepmc + cancer_gene_census); gwas_get_snps_for_gene (50 SNPs mapped to KDR/4q12, no trait annotation)

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker returns 64 human records for KDR; the marker is overwhelmingly a normal vascular/endothelial marker (endothelial cells, endothelial progenitor cells, hematopoietic stem cells across blood, blood vessel, kidney, liver, lung, heart, eye, etc.), with only a few cancer-cell records (bone cancer stem cell, breast/stomach tumor endothelial cell). No melanoma-specific or melanocyte-restricted expression is reported, indicating poor tumor-cell-intrinsic tissue selectivity.
right_tissue.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene (operation=search_by_gene, gene_symbol=KDR, species=Human; 64 records, majority 'Normal cell' endothelial)

right_safety.finding: OpenFDA reports 15,246 adverse-event cases for the VEGFR2 inhibitor lenvatinib; the top case (report 10066620, serious/life-threatening) shows cardiac failure, toxic nephropathy, raised creatinine/urea, hypertension-related and GI/constitutional events at 18 mg QD. This class toxicity is consistent with KDR being a normal endothelial/vascular marker (CellMarker: endothelial cells in heart, kidney, lung), flagging on-target cardiovascular and renal liability.
right_safety.provenance: OpenFDA_search_drug_events (drug_name=lenvatinib; total=15246 reports); corroborated by CellMarker normal endothelial expression

right_patient.finding: search_clinical_trials returns 9 melanoma trials using lenvatinib (VEGFR2/KDR inhibitor), including two completed Phase 3 studies of lenvatinib + pembrolizumab as first-line advanced melanoma (NCT03820986 LEAP-003 and its China extension NCT04889118), plus Phase 2 studies in uveal melanoma (NCT05308901) and anti-PD-1-exposed melanoma (NCT03776136 LEAP-004). Substantial, clinically advanced patient program in melanoma.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials (intervention=lenvatinib, condition=melanoma; 9 studies incl. 2 Phase 3 completed)

right_commercial.finding: TheraSAbDab lists 6 antibody therapeutics against KDR/CD309/VEGFR2: ramucirumab (Approved), pulocimab (Phase 3, active), olinvacimab (Phase 2, active), inlecitug (2025, TBC), plus discontinued alacizumab and vulinacimab. KDR is a validated, commercially crowded target (one approved mAb) with an active melanoma small-molecule program (lenvatinib), indicating a competitive rather than white-space opportunity.
right_commercial.provenance: TheraSAbDab_search_by_target (target=KDR; 6 therapeutics, 1 approved); search_clinical_trials melanoma program

tractability.finding: Pharos classifies KDR (UniProt P35968) as Tclin, the highest development level (existing approved drugs modulate this target), family Kinase (type III receptor tyrosine kinase), with 1038 publications and novelty 0.00016. Highly tractable and clinically drugged.
